summary: Backend + Frontend regression for the new Balance Confirmation Dashboard (run e96d4d22-f7be-458b-a314-abb97acddf55, client cli_8656f99622ae). All 7 backend pytest cases pass and every spec'd UI element + interaction works. Highlights: (a) /api/.../analytics returns the exact 6 status buckets (confirmed/reconciled/disputed/in_flight/failed/not_sent) with correct sum-consistency, (b) categories include trade_receivable/trade_payable/bank/unsecured_loans + 'other', (c) funnel is 6-stage Identified→With-email→Dispatched→Delivered→Opened→Responded, (d) summary.pdf returns valid %PDF >5KB, (e) summary.xlsx returns valid PK ZIP, (f) reconciled-bucket count == top-level reconciled_count, (g) UI shows Dashboard|Workbench switcher with Dashboard default, all data-testids present (hero ×4, category-matrix + 4 cat cards, funnel ×6 + wrapper, status-donut SVG renders via Recharts, top-disputed/unresponsive panels, subhead heatmap with 12 rows), (h) Summary PDF & Summary XLSX live INSIDE bc-dashboard-header (verified with hdr.contains()) and the old inline run-header buttons are gone, (i) clicking the buttons triggers the corresponding 200 response with correct content-type. On the demo run the response data is sparse (1 in_flight, 837 not_sent, 0 confirmed/reconciled/disputed) so the reconciled-from-recon-comments path is exercised in code but evaluates to 0 here — that is expected per the request and confirmed via /analytics JSON.

issues: Hero card testid for 'Audit Coverage · by ₹' is auto-slugified to `bc-hero-audit-coverage---by--` (trailing double-dash because ₹ becomes empty). Functional, but ugly for automation. Consider using an explicit testid like `bc-hero-audit-coverage-amount` instead of deriving it from the label.

Recharts logs 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' twice on first paint of the status donut — happens because ResponsiveContainer measures before layout settles. Non-blocking but consider giving the donut wrapper an explicit minHeight to silence the warning.

React hydration console error: 'whitespace text nodes cannot be a child of <colgroup>' — points to whitespace between <col> tags in one of the workbench tables (not the dashboard). Cosmetic / hydration warning only.

verified.analytics_status_buckets: {confirmed, reconciled, disputed, in_flight, failed, not_sent} — exact match
verified.analytics_reconciled_consistency: overall.by_status.reconciled.count == reconciled_count (both 0 for this run, expected)
verified.funnel_stages: 6 — Identified, With email, Dispatched, Delivered, Opened, Responded
verified.ui_view_switcher: bc-view-dashboard (default active) and bc-view-workbench both clickable; clicking workbench hides bc-dashboard and reveals tables; clicking dashboard restores it.
verified.ui_dashboard_testids: bc-dashboard, bc-dashboard-header, bc-summary-xlsx, bc-summary-pdf, bc-dashboard-refresh, bc-category-matrix, bc-cat-trade_receivable/trade_payable/bank/unsecured_loans, bc-status-donut (svg present), bc-top-disputed, bc-top-unresponsive, bc-subhead-heatmap (12 rows), bc-hero-* ×4, bc-funnel-{identified,with-email,dispatched,delivered,opened,responded} — all present
verified.downloads_inside_dashboard_header: bc-summary-pdf and bc-summary-xlsx are descendants of bc-dashboard-header (hdr.contains(...) === true). Only one of each exists on the page (old inline run-header buttons removed).
verified.download_clicks: Both PDF (200 application/pdf) and XLSX (200 .../spreadsheetml.sheet) download responses captured; toast 'PDF downloaded' visible.

action_items: (Cosmetic) Hardcode hero KPI testid for 'Audit Coverage · by ₹' instead of slug-deriving it (currently `bc-hero-audit-coverage---by--`).
(Cosmetic) Suppress Recharts width(-1) warning on initial paint by giving the status-donut container an explicit minHeight or using fixed pixel height in SummaryDashboard.jsx.
(Cosmetic) Hydration error in workbench tables — clean up whitespace inside <colgroup>.

Consider seeding a small QA fixture (a few responses with decision=disputed + a recon comment) so the reconciled bucket can be visually validated end-to-end on the demo run; today the dashboard panels are populated but every status row except not_sent/in_flight is zero.
critical_code_review_comments: analytics.py is well-structured and small (~340 lines) — single source of truth for both /analytics JSON and PDF mirror. Good separation.

_reconciled_ledger_ids correctly uses response_id linkage to bc_recon_comments. Edge case: if a comment has response_id but no auditor text it still flips disputed→reconciled; consider requiring non-empty comment body before promoting.
_top_unresponsive uses _STALE_SENT_DAYS=7 hardcoded — fine, but worth surfacing as a request-level config later.
summary_export.build_summary_pdf accepting analytics is the right move for SSOT consistency between dashboard and PDF.
Frontend uses Recharts (CRA/Webpack-friendly), consistent with the 'no import.meta' constraint.
seed_data_creation: None — used the provided demo run e96d4d22-f7be-458b-a314-abb97acddf55. State untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend dashboard analytics + summary downloads are fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_balance_confirmation_dashboard.py and pass against the qa-bot run. Frontend dashboard at /dashboard/clients/cli_8656f99622ae/utilities/balance-confirmation/runs/e96d4d22-f7be-458b-a314-abb97acddf55 has all required data-testids and interactions wired. The demo run has 838 ledgers but only 1 in_flight + 837 not_sent — to exercise the disputed/reconciled visuals you'll need a run with submitted responses + recon comments. Three cosmetic warnings (hero testid slug, Recharts width=-1, colgroup whitespace hydration) are noted but non-blocking.
